HONASA CONSUMER : WINS ARBITRATION AWARD AGAINST RSM IN DISTRIBUTOR DISPUTE ⚖️🌿

• 🏛️ Sole Arbitrator Justice (Retd.) Hrishikesh Roy passed final award in favour of Honasa Consumer against RSM General Trading LLC

• 🇦🇪 Tribunal ruled that RSM breached the arbitration agreement, exclusive jurisdiction clause and governing law clause under the ADA

• 🚫 RSM restrained from initiating or continuing proceedings before Dubai Courts or any other forum in breach of dispute resolution terms

• 💰 Honasa awarded AED 1.56 Mn (~₹3.5 Cr) towards litigation costs incurred in Dubai & allied proceedings in India

• 💵 Company also awarded AED 1.06 Mn towards substitution costs and AED 4.34 Mn towards loss of profits arising from RSM’s breaches

• 🇮🇳 Tribunal additionally awarded INR 76.5 lakh towards arbitration proceeding costs

• ⏳ Post-award interest applicable if dues remain unpaid after 30 days

• ⚖️ Earlier Dubai compensation claim against Honasa had been reduced from AED 25 Mn to AED 1.7 Mn by Dubai Court of Appeal; final Cassation Court judgment scheduled on 17 June 2026

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major legal relief for Honasa with arbitration award in its favour, recovery of costs/damages and reduced legal uncertainty)

**INDIA’S ELECTRICITY DISTRIBUTION CAPEX CYCLE MAY HAVE TURNED UP AGAIN ⚡️📈**

- Electricity distribution capex cycle showed strong revival signs in FY26 after nearly four years of contraction  
- Key indicators surged:
  - New project announcements ▲ 8.4x YoY  
  - Projects under implementation ▲ 4.6x YoY  
  - Outstanding project pipeline ▲ 3.5x YoY  

- Previous cycle peaked during FY16–FY20:
  - FY16 announcements reached ₹13,010 Cr across 13 projects  
  - Outstanding pipeline crossed ₹2 lakh Cr  
  - Projects under implementation peaked at 76 worth ₹13,330 Cr in FY20  

- By FY25, the cycle had almost collapsed:
  - Only 3 announcements worth ₹64 Cr  
  - 5 projects under implementation worth ₹144 Cr  
  - Outstanding pipeline reduced to 7 projects worth ₹197 Cr  
  - Project completions were zero in FY25  

- FY26 marked clear reversal with project completions resuming:
  - 3 completions vs 0 in FY25  

- Possible drivers behind the revival:
  - RDSS rollout acceleration  
  - Discom restructuring  
  - Renewable energy integration needs  
  - T&D infrastructure expansion  
  - Rising electricity demand  

- Distribution infrastructure historically remained weakest link in India’s power ecosystem due to:
  - High AT&C losses  
  - Weak discom finances  
  - Underinvestment in grid infrastructure  

- Sustained revival could benefit:
  - Power distribution EPC companies  
  - Cable & conductor manufacturers  
  - Transformer makers  
  - Smart metering ecosystem  
  - Grid automation players  
  - T&D infrastructure companies  

- Simultaneous increase in:
  - Project count  
  - Project value  
  - Execution activity  

strongly suggests a new capex cycle may be forming in India’s power distribution sector  

**Final Takeaway**
- India’s electricity distribution segment appears to be entering a fresh investment cycle driven by grid modernization, renewable integration and discom reforms. If execution sustains, distribution infrastructure could become one of the next major long-duration themes within the broader Indian power capex story  

**Impact**
- Positive Long-Term Structural Theme

HSBC om Voltas

Buy, TP Rs 1500

FY26 results: 4Q margins disappoint more than topline miss, mainly from increased commodity cost and higher FX

However, pricing action and cost reduction measures should usher in a gradual recovery in coming quarters

Key takeaways from earnings call,

1) RAC outlook: management expects improved performance versus last year, supported by strong April–May demand.

2) Pricing: highlighted double-digit input cost inflation (including commodities and FX) & indicated further price increases will be implemented progressively as higher costs flow through.

3) Margin trajectory: gradual improvement, moving towards FY25 level over time.

4) CAC growth: CAC is positioned as a key medium-term growth driver, supported by infrastructure build-out and rising demand from sectors such as manufacturing and data centres.

5) VoltBek: Continued focus on premiumisation, innovation, localisation, and distribution expansion to improve penetration & operating leverage over medium term

reports.text:
CLSA on USL

Hold, TP Rs 1238

4QFY26 volume decline of 5.6% YoY, 4% below expectation.

P&A volume declined by 3.1% YoY and popular category volume declined by 16.1% YoY, largely due to the impact of Maharashtra Made liquor.

Net sales grew 3.4% and came in 4.5%/2.3 below expectations, which was already known.

Gross margin expanded by 281bps YoY and came in 92bps/104bps above our/consensus estimates.

However, underlying gross margin expanded by 212 bps YoY and Ebitda margins expanded by 226bps YoY driven by gross margin expansion.

reports.text:
JPM on USL

OW, TP Rs 1445

Delivered a solid beat on Q4 EBITDA despite relatively unexciting revenue growth (which was disclosed earlier)

Positive surprise was led by gross margin delivery coming ahead of expectations at 47.3% (+210bp y/y), which is a multi-quarter high besides lower-than-anticipated A&P spends (-5% y/y) and employee costs (+2% y/y).

Further, management narrative sounds positive regarding growth trajectory in FY27, noting a strong set up for FY27 enabled by progressive policy interventions in Karnataka (steep price reductions for P&A portfolio) and potential benefits from UK FTA.

PITTI ENGINEERING : ANNOUNCES MAJOR CASTING CAPACITY EXPANSION PLAN ⚙️🏭

• 🔧 Ongoing debottlenecking capex to increase casting capacity from 18,600 MT to 24,600 MT by H1FY27

• 💰 Around ₹100 Cr capex already incurred; facilities expected to become operational by end of H1FY27

• 💍 Studded jewellery contribution moderated, leading to ~30 bps margin dilution as diamond/studded products carry higher margins

• 🎉 Higher festive discounts & market expansion offers during Gudi Padwa impacted margins by ~50 bps

• 🚫 Company clarified refinery business was fully discontinued from Sept 30, 2024; all gold bar & coin sales are purely customer-driven

• 🔄 QoQ margin moderation also driven by higher franchise mix rising from 8% to 12%, impacting margins by ~50 bps

• 🪙 Gold bars & coins mix increased sequentially from 35% to 40%, causing another ~50 bps pressure

• 💎 Studded jewellery ratio declined from 8.2% to 7.3%, impacting margins by ~40 bps QoQ

• ✨ Q3 FY26 had benefited from strong sales of premium antique jewellery during Diwali & Dussehra, creating a high-margin base effect of ~270 bps

• 📈 Management said promotional spending & expansion offers are strategic investments aimed at increasing brand visibility, footfalls and long-term growth

reports.text:
Sheela Foam: The mattress maker's board has reappointed Rahul Gautam as CMD, Rakesh Chahar as Deputy MD, and Tushaar Gautam as Vice Chairman and Joint MD, all for 5-year terms.

Kirloskar Oil Engines: The company plans to deploy a massive capital expenditure of Rs 1,400 crore to enhance manufacturing capacity at its unit in Maharashtra.

Network People (NPST): The digital banking solutions provider has secured new implementation orders from three cooperative banks for its specialized 'Bank-in-a-Box' platform.

Caplin Point: The pharmaceutical company's arm received a crucial US FDA nod for its Foscarnet Sodium Injection, a medication used to treat CMV retinitis in AIDS patients.

Andhra Paper: The company has re-appointed Saurabh Bangur as Managing Director for a 5-year term beginning October 1.

Pitti Engineering: The company's board has approved a Rs 290 crore capital expenditure plan to establish a new greenfield casting unit.

Cemindia Projects: The company has fixed June 12 as the record date to determine eligibility for its final dividend of Rs 3 per share.

ICICI Lombard General Insurance: The general insurer has set May 29 as the record date for its final dividend payout of Rs 7 per share.

HCLTech: The IT services giant has entered into a strategic partnership with Red Hat to co-develop and deploy advanced AI infrastructure solutions.

GSP Crop Science: The agrochemical company plans to acquire the remaining 21% stake in its subsidiary, GSP Intermediates.

ICICI Prudential Life Insurance: The life insurer has fixed June 5 as the record date for its final dividend of Rs 1.65 per share.

Great Eastern Shipping: The shipping major has successfully delivered its Medium Range Tanker, 'Jag Pankhi', to buyers as per a sale contracted earlier.

Tamilnad Mercantile Bank: The bank received a major relief via a rectification order from the Tamil Nadu tax body, which slashed an existing tax order to just Rs 3 crore from a massive Rs 204 crore.

Pricol: The automotive components manufacturer announced that Vanitha Mohan has resigned as Chairman, and the board has appointed current MD Vikram Mohan to take over the role. The company also approved extending a corporate guarantee of up to Rs 150 crore to a subsidiary.

Creative Newtech: Following the resignation of Abhijit Kanvinde on April 14, the company has officially appointed Ajit Thakur as its new Chief Financial Officer.

Adani Power: The company issued a clarification stating that while the GVK bid has obtained CCI clearance, it is still under evaluation and the acquisition has not yet received approval from the Committee of Creditors (CoC).

Sagar Cements: The cement maker has successfully commissioned a 2.80 MW Waste Heat Recovery Power System (WHRS) in Andhra Pradesh, with an additional 1.55 MW capacity slated for July.

Indian Overseas Bank: The state-run lender has reduced its Base Rate by 10 basis points down to 9.70%.

Jyoti Structures: The power transmission company is expanding its international portfolio, signing a joint venture contract to develop a 220kV transmission line in Sri Lanka.

Navkar Corp: The logistics firm has received tax immunity for AY25, as the IT department granted relief from an earlier penalty order.

Gujarat Energy: The Registrar of Companies (RoC) Ahmedabad has officially approved the company's name change from Gujarat Gas to Gujarat Energy.

SBI: India's largest lender will hold its 71st Annual General Meeting (AGM) on June 18.

FACT: The fertilizers company is preparing to start DAP production at its Udyogamandal Plant in Kerala from May 15, following the necessary approvals from the Fertiliser Department.

GMR Power: The company has extended a total corporate guarantee of Rs 240 crore to back three of its step-down smart meter subsidiaries, providing each with an Rs 80 crore working capital line.

Shalby: The hospital chain announced that the RoC has struck off its step-down arm, Ningen Lifecare.

**Stocks in News**

Capri Global Capital: The NBFC announced it has successfully crossed a major milestone by establishing over 1,000 gold loan branches across India.

Bharat Bijlee: The company has revised the record date for its upcoming dividend payment to July 15.

M&M Financial: The NBFC's board has approved plans to raise up to Rs 3,000 crore through the issuance of Non-Convertible Debentures (NCDs).

NTPC Green: The company's subsidiary has declared the Commercial Operation Date (COD) for two solar units in Rajasthan (12.5 MW and 50 MW capacities), which will officially go live on May 15.

Patel Engineering: The infrastructure firm is set to offload its entire stake in ACP Tollways for a total consideration of Rs 55 crore.

Akums Drugs: The pharmaceutical manufacturer reported that operations at certain manufacturing sites in Haridwar are currently disrupted due to labour unrest at the SIDCUL industrial area.

Muthoot Finance: The gold loan financier has appointed George M George, George M Jacob, George Alexander, and Eapen Alexander as Whole-Time Directors.

HDB Financial: The company has appointed Natarajan Srinivasan as its Non-Executive Chairman and set June 18 as the record date for a dividend of Rs 2 per share.

Indian Metals: The company has terminated its 40 MW hybrid renewable supply agreement with Ampin Energy Utility citing project and approval delays, and expects its Rs 12.32 crore investment to be refunded.

FUJIYAMA POWER SYSTEMS LTD Q4FY26 HIGHLIGHTS ☀️⚡

Q4 FY26 Performance
- Revenue: ₹900.8 Cr (+87.5% YoY | +53.1% QoQ)  
- EBITDA: ₹171.5 Cr (+116.9% YoY | +56.0% QoQ)

- PAT: ₹106.3 Cr (+107.5% YoY | +58.0% QoQ)  
- EPS: ₹3.58 vs ₹1.83 (+95.6% YoY)  

FY26 Performance
- Revenue: ₹2,654.5 Cr (+72.3% YoY)  
- EBITDA: ₹490.3 Cr (+97.3% YoY)  
- EBITDA Margin: 18.5% vs 16.1% YoY  
- PAT: ₹304.1 Cr (+94.5% YoY)  
- EPS: ₹10.24 vs ₹5.59 (+83.2% YoY)  

Distribution Expansion
- Added during Q4:
  - 80+ distributors  
  - 450 dealers  
  - 30 exclusive shops  

- Total channel partners crossed 8,900+  

Capacity Expansion
- Commissioned:
  - 2,000 MW solar panel manufacturing capacity at Ratlam  

- Setting up:
  - 1,200 MW TOPCon solar cell facility at Ratlam  
  - Planned investment of ~₹350 Cr  

Operational Updates
- Margin expansion driven by:
  - Higher operating scale  
  - Better plant utilisation  
  - Backward integration benefits  

- Delays in power electronics and battery capacities due to:
  - Lithium-ion technology integration  
  - Geopolitical supply disruptions  

- Inverter line expected in Q1FY27  
- Battery manufacturing expected in Q2FY27  

Management Commentary
- FY26 described as milestone year post IPO  
- Growth driven by:
  - Rooftop solar expansion  
  - Residential solar demand  
  - Tier-2 & Tier-3 market penetration  
  - Government policy support  

- Management remains optimistic on:
  - PM Surya Ghar demand  
  - Policy continuity  
  - Long-term residential solar adoption  

Final Takeaway
- Fujiyama Power Systems delivered strong growth supported by rooftop solar demand, distribution expansion and aggressive backward integration. New manufacturing capacities and improving operating leverage position the company strongly for long-term growth in India’s residential solar ecosystem  

Impact
- Positive

IRB INFRASTRUCTURE TRUST : OFFERS ₹4,663 CR BOT HIGHWAY ASSETS TO IRB INVIT FUND 🛣️💰

• 🏗️ IRB Infrastructure Trust (Private InvIT) offered to sell 2 BOT highway assets to IRB InvIT Fund (Public InvIT)

• 📍 Assets include: • Solapur-Yedeshi NH211 (Maharashtra) – 395 lane kms
• Chittorgarh-Gulabpura NH79 (Rajasthan) – 749 lane kms

• 📏 Total portfolio size: 1,144 lane kms

• 💵 Combined Enterprise Value stands at ~₹4,663 Cr

• 🔄 Transaction part of IRB’s B.E.S.T. (Bid, Execute, Stabilize, Transfer) strategy to recycle capital from mature assets into new growth opportunities

• 📈 Management says transfer will enhance IRB InvIT Fund’s asset portfolio, weighted average concession life & revenue visibility

• 🎯 IRB Group targets achieving ~₹1.4 lakh Cr asset base by 2029

• 🛣️ Earlier, IRB had already transferred 3 BOT assets and 1 HAM asset to IRB InvIT Fund

• 📊 Post transfer of these additional assets, IRB InvIT Fund’s Enterprise Value may rise to ~₹23,000 Cr, subject to approvals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Asset monetisation improves capital recycling, strengthens InvIT portfolio and supports future growth pipeline)

reports.text:
LT FOODS : Q4 MARGIN PRESSURE DRIVEN BY TEMPORARY & STRATEGIC FACTORS 🌾📉

• 🇺🇸 U.S. tariff pass-through impacted reported margins; ₹229 Cr tariff added to both revenue & COGS in Q4 at zero margin

• 📊 Tariff impact alone reduced Q4 FY26 EBITDA margin by ~230 bps, though no impact on absolute EBITDA in ₹ terms

• 📦 Inventory build-up costs and partial tariff absorption in the U.S. temporarily affected profitability

• 📢 Brand & marketing investments increased QoQ — US spend up ₹20 Cr, UK spend up ₹3 Cr

• 🇬🇧 LT Foods UK business remains in investment phase despite revenue growth; employee cost surged 479% QoQ due to headcount expansion

• 🏪 UK distribution expansion continues toward long-term £100 Mn revenue target

• 🌱 Organic segment EBITDA fell 72% QoQ to ₹16 Cr due to product mix changes, countervailing duty case fees & transition costs

• 🔄 Organic business undergoing strategic remodel for next growth phase; management expects margin recovery as new model matures

KRN Heat Exchanger & Refrigeration (KRN) - Q4 & FY26 Results

Good business. Concerning balance sheet. Two very different stories in the same filing.

Revenue Q4: ₹179.5 Cr (+36.5% YoY, +17.1% QoQ) | FY26: ₹600.1 Cr (+39.6% YoY)

Met guidance at the lower end of ₹600–620 Cr. Consolidated revenue is actually lower than standalone (₹676 Cr) - not an error. The parent sells components to subsidiary KRN HVAC Products; those ₹76 Cr of intra-group sales get eliminated in consolidation. 

The subsidiary itself generated ₹219 Cr in external revenue and ₹6.1 Cr PAT in its first full year - entirely new business that didn't exist in FY25.

Domestic = 84.5% of Q4 revenue, growing at 39.6% YoY. 
Exports = 15.5%, growing at 21.7% YoY but slipping 2.6% QoQ. Domestic is running away from exports.

EBITDA Margin  Q4: 18.49% (+458 bps YoY, −155 bps QoQ vs Q3's 20.04%) | FY26: 18.45% (+249 bps YoY)

The YoY improvement is genuine - backward integration is working. Raw material costs fell 44% YoY in Q4 despite revenue up 36.5%. That's structural, not a one-quarter fluke.

The QoQ dip is the concern. Q3 briefly crossed management's 20% target; Q4 retreated on product mix shift and new facility fixed costs (depreciation +405% YoY) spreading over a softer revenue base. FY26 ended 155 bps short of the stated target.

Normalized PAT FY26: ₹76.8 Cr (+45.2% YoY) | Q4: ₹23.4 Cr (+57.2% YoY)

One nuance: PAT growth (+44.6%) beat PBT growth (+31.8%) because the effective tax rate collapsed from 28.9% to 21.9% - deferred tax benefits from the new facility plus a ₹3 Cr prior-period tax reversal in Q4. Strip that out and underlying earnings growth is closer to ~30%. Still solid, but the beat has a tax tailwind embedded in it.

The working capital problem - the real story

Operating profit before WC changes: +₹118 Cr Net CFO after WC: −₹114 Cr

₹232 Cr of working capital consumed in one year. Where: 
→ Inventories: +₹177 Cr (stock jumped ₹96 Cr → ₹273 Cr, +185% YoY) 
→ Trade receivables: +₹82 Cr (+88% vs revenue +40% — outpacing) 
→ Trade payables: +₹103 Cr (partial offset - suppliers extending credit)

The inventory build is deliberate. Management is pre-stocking for new product lines - bus AC, refrigeration units, bar-and-plate heat exchangers — that require different inputs and longer lead times than legacy heat exchangers. The bet: stock now, sell in FY27.

The problem: no clear timeline for when ₹273 Cr converts to cash. Short-term borrowings exploded from ₹32 Cr to ₹187 Cr (+484%) to fund it. Current ratio: 4.52× (FY25) → 1.64× (FY26). Not a crisis yet but the direction in a single year is steep.

Export gap - structural, not a rounding error

Guidance: 30–35% export share by FY26 | Actual: 16.6% - a ~14 pp miss. And declining QoQ (Q3: 18.7% → Q4: 15.5%). Domestic demand is simply growing faster than international conversion.

Why it matters: export customers pay higher prices → better margins; PLI incentives (₹141.72 Cr sanctioned) are likely linked to export thresholds. Management has now shifted goalposts to "50% export share in 3 years" - conceding FY26 was a miss. The April 2026 ₹55 Cr export order is the first concrete sign of traction, but one order doesn't move the needle yet.

Guidance scorecard
Revenue ₹600–620 Cr ✅ 
EBITDA ~20% ⚠️ (18.45%) 
Export share 30–35% ❌ (16.6%) 
WC normalization ❌ (CFO −₹114 Cr)

Balance sheet 
D/E: 0.07× → 0.33× YoY | Net worth +15.1% to ₹574 Cr | PPE +268% as new facility capitalizes | IPO proceeds fully deployed | QIP up to ₹500 Cr approved April 2026 - more capital raising likely ahead.

Watch for FY27
→ Does the ₹273 Cr inventory unwind or does the bus AC ramp (₹160 Cr guided) add more before it normalizes? This determines whether CFO turns positive or stays negative for a second year. 

→ Can export share move from 16.6% toward 30% on the ₹55 Cr April order + PLI tailwind? Or does domestic demand keep crowding it out?

Note: This is not investment advice.

FUJIYAMA POWER; UTL SOLAR PROVIDES UPDATE ON BAWAL PLANT FIRE INCIDENT 🔥🏭

• ⚠️ Fire incident occurred at company’s Bawal manufacturing facility in Haryana

• 🙏 No casualties or injuries reported among employees or workers

• 🔋 Facility primarily engaged in manufacturing lead-acid batteries with installed capacity of 1.3 GW

• 📦 Inventory at facility estimated at ~₹105 crore; fixed assets value (excluding land) around ₹90 crore

• ⚡ Preliminary findings indicate possible electrical short circuit as likely trigger for fire

• 🛡️ Company maintains comprehensive insurance coverage for affected facility

• ⛔ Operations at Bawal plant temporarily suspended

• 🏭 Company has engaged third-party manufacturers to ensure supply continuity & minimise customer disruption

• 💻 ERP systems and critical business operations remain fully functional and secure

• 👨‍🏭 Employees being offered transfer options to other manufacturing plants to maintain employment continuity

• 📢 Management says incident unlikely to have any material long-term impact on overall business operations

🟡 Impact: Mixed (Temporary operational disruption due to fire, though insurance cover and alternate manufacturing arrangements may limit long-term financial impact)

• 🇮🇳 DGFT inserts new conditions under SION norms for gold imports with immediate effect

• ⚖️ Advance Authorisation (AA) for gold imports now capped at maximum 100 kilograms per authorisation

• 🏭 First-time applicants to undergo mandatory physical inspection of manufacturing facility

• 📦 Fresh gold import approvals allowed only after fulfilling at least 50% export obligation under previous authorisations

• 📑 Importers required to submit fortnightly performance reports certified by Chartered Accountant

• 🏢 Regional Authorities to submit monthly consolidated monitoring reports to DGFT Headquarters

• 🔍 Move aimed at tighter monitoring, compliance oversight & curbing misuse in gold import/export ecosystem

🟡 Impact: Mixed (Could tighten gold import flows and increase compliance burden for jewellery exporters, while improving regulatory oversight)

NISUS FINANCE : INVESTS ₹90 CR IN PUNE RESIDENTIAL PROJECT 🏙️💰

• 🏗️ Nisus Finance invested ₹90 Cr through RESO-I Fund in Paranjape Group’s residential project at Hinjewadi Phase 1, Pune

• 🌆 Project is part of “Blue Ridge” — a 150-acre integrated township with 33 residential towers and 6,000+ families already residing

• 💼 Township also houses 3 SEZs employing 35,000+ IT professionals along with schools, commercial spaces & modern amenities

• 🏢 Project includes 188 premium apartments with 3 BHK & 4 BHK configurations

• 📐 Total saleable area stands at 4.02 lakh sq ft across 1.09 acres of land

• 💵 Project has estimated GDV of ~₹370 Cr and expected operating surplus of ~₹143 Cr

• 📈 Nisus said Hinjewadi continues to witness strong end-user housing demand driven by IT employment & robust infrastructure

• 🤝 Company highlighted partnership with Paranjape Group, which has 35+ years legacy and 2+ million sq ft delivery track record

• 🛡️ Investment structured with focus on capital protection, strong cash-flow visibility & risk-adjusted returns

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Nisus Finance’s real estate portfolio with exposure to a high-demand Pune residential micro-market and premium housing demand trend)
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
PRITIKA AUTO : HC SETS ASIDE LAND AUCTION, REFUND ORDERED ⚖️🏭

• 🏗️ Company had earlier emerged as highest bidder for 64 Kanal land & building at Hoshiarpur, Punjab

• 📍 Property was intended for future expansion plans of the company

• ⚠️ Company later learnt that litigation was pending regarding the property

• 🏛️ Pritika Auto approached Hon’ble Punjab & Haryana High Court seeking cancellation of auction to avoid legal complications

• 📄 High Court accepted the request and set aside the auction vide order dated May 11, 2026

• 💰 Court also ordered refund of money deposited by the company

• ❌ Company clarified that the transaction was not a related party transaction

🟡 Impact: Neutral (Expansion-related acquisition cancelled due to litigation concerns; avoids legal risk but delays potential capacity expansion plans)

reports.text:
PN GADGIL Q4 ; Management says FY26 was a landmark year for P N Gadgil Jewellers, with revenue crossing the ₹10,000 Cr milestone for the first time, growing 39.6% YoY to ₹10,739 Cr. 

Growth was driven by strong wedding/festive demand, new collections, and rising preference for lightweight and studded jewellery.

Profitability improved sharply, with EBITDA up 89.6% and PAT rising 87.8% YoY, supported by better product mix and operating leverage. Retail remained the key growth driver, while Franchise and E-commerce segments grew 83% and 105% respectively, reflecting strong omni-channel execution.

reports.text:
JSW STEEL Q4 🏭 India’s crude steel production rose 10.8% YoY to 44.68 MnT in Q4 FY26; FY26 production up 11.2% to 169.2 MnT

• 📈 Steel consumption increased 10.4% YoY to 44.61 MnT in Q4; FY26 demand up 7.9% to 164 MnT

• 🔥 Domestic steel demand accelerated sharply in Q4; March 2026 demand crossed record 16 MnT

• 🛡️ Safeguard duty helped curb imports; steel imports declined ~22% YoY in FY26

• 🌍 Steel exports jumped ~33% YoY, making India a net steel exporter again after two years

• 🇨🇳 China steel production fell 4.6% YoY in Jan-Mar CY26, reducing output by nearly 12 MnT

• 📉 China steel exports (including semis) declined 8.1% YoY to ~27.5 MnT in Jan-Mar CY26
reports.channel: Beat The Street News| Latest Share Market News

reports.text:
JSW STEEL Q4 : RECORD SALES & EBITDA PERFORMANCE 🚀⚙️

• 🏭 Consolidated steel production stood at 7.49 MnT; up 0.1% QoQ, down 2% YoY

• 📈 Steel sales hit record 7.97 MnT; up 4% QoQ and 6% YoY

• 🇮🇳 Domestic sales also touched record 7.09 MnT; up 8% QoQ and 6% YoY

• 🏢 Institutional sales volumes rose 7% QoQ and 5% YoY; Retail sales up 6% QoQ and 3% YoY

• 🌍 Exports surged 36% YoY to 0.75 MnT, contributing 10% of Indian operations sales

• 💰 Revenue from Operations hit highest-ever ₹51,180 Cr

• 🔥 Adjusted EBITDA reached record ₹9,713 Cr with strong 19% EBITDA margin

• 📊 EBITDA jumped 47% QoQ, aided by better steel realizations despite higher coking coal prices

• 🧾 Reported PAT came at ₹19,243 Cr, boosted by exceptional gain of ₹17,888 Cr from BPSL slump sale

STALLION INDIA Q4 : The company’s proposed 10,000 MT R-32 manufacturing facility in Bhilwara, Rajasthan remains a key long-term growth driver and is on track for commissioning by October 2026. 

The project will strengthen backward integration, supply-chain security, and import substitution, while benefiting from the shift toward low-GWP refrigerants.

During FY26, the company also expanded its presence in specialty gases, semiconductor gases, helium, and HFO infrastructure.

reports.text:
STALLION INDIA Q4 : Co says company faced a sharp ~50% increase in raw material prices since March 2026 due to geopolitical disruptions and global supply volatility.

 While challenges persist, the company has implemented calibrated price hikes and product mix optimisation to protect competitiveness and maintain stable operations.

WEPS SOLUTIONS – CESTAT ORDER UPDATE ⚖️

• WeP Solutions Limited receives favourable ruling from CESTAT, Bangalore

• Customs, Excise & Service Tax Appellate Tribunal sets aside entire service tax demand

• Dispute related to ₹4,25,14,108 service tax for period April 2011 to June 2012

• Case pertained to “Supply of Tangible Goods” category under managed printing services

• Tribunal order removes associated interest and penalty as well

• Company confirms no financial, operational, or business impact from the ruling

• Amount was already disclosed as contingent liability in financial statements

MCX – SEBI EASE OF DOING BUSINESS PROPOSALS 📑⚖️

• Multi Commodity Exchange of India impacted by SEBI consultation paper on simplifying exchange-traded derivatives regulations

• SEBI proposes merging multiple master circulars for stock exchanges and commodity derivatives segments

• Aim is to remove duplicate and obsolete compliance requirements across exchanges and clearing corporations

• Proposal to remove “Close To Money (CTM)” option series norms in commodity derivatives for simpler exercise mechanism

• Exchanges may receive greater flexibility in monitoring position limits and operational compliance

• Product Advisory Committee (PAC) meeting frequency for non-agri commodities may be reduced to once a year

• Daily newspaper/media disclosure requirements for derivatives transactions may be discontinued; exchange website disclosure to continue

• Compliance framework for trading software, surveillance, risk management, and clearing corporations to be streamlined

• Overall objective is to reduce compliance burden and improve ease of doing business for market infrastructure institutions

Quality Power (QPOWER) - Q4 FY26

Q4 EBITDA margin dipped to 21.1%, below the 22% floor. Before you react, read what caused it.

Q4 numbers

Q4 revenue: ₹281 Cr, up 159.3% YoY and down 1.1% QoQ - flat sequentially, which is fine. 

Q4 EBITDA margin: 21.1% vs Q3's 27.9%, a 680 bps sequential drop. Q4 normalized PAT: ₹73 Cr, +139% YoY. The dip was almost entirely one item.

The Q4 margin: read the footnote

Endoks Enerji (Turkey subsidiary) recorded a ₹25.7 Cr hyperinflation loss under IndAS 29 in Q4. This is classified under Other Expenses - which is why Other Expenses surged 234% QoQ (₹17.6 Cr → ₹58.9 Cr). Hyperinflation accounting is a non-cash monetary asset adjustment for operating in a hyperinflationary economy. 

Endoks itself runs at ~30% operating margins. The business is healthy - Turkey macro is the issue, not the operations. Separately, material costs ticked up 210 bps as a percentage of Q4 revenue, commodity input pressure that is real and worth tracking. These two together explain the Q4 miss.

FY26 in context

Full year: revenue ₹947 Cr vs guided ₹700–850 Cr - beat the upper end by ₹97 Cr. FY26 EBITDA margin: 24.9% - above the 22% floor. 

Mehru EBITDA margin reached ~20% in Q4, ahead of the 16–17% target management set at acquisition. Normalized PAT FY26: ₹207 Cr, +107% YoY. Every metric beat guidance on a full-year basis. The Q4 dip is noise against a strong year.

Working capital: the one real concern

CFO/PAT FY26: 0.43x. Trade receivables absorbed ₹106 Cr, 40% of operating cash flow. Inventory built ₹14 Cr. This is project-execution working capital - milestone billing creates lags. The balance sheet is clean (D/E 4.6%), current ratio 2.49x, so there's no stress. But cash conversion needs to improve in FY27.

Guidance scorecard:

Revenue ₹700–850 Cr → ₹947 Cr ✅
EBITDA margin floor 22% → FY26: 24.9% ✅ · Q4: 21.1% ⚠
Mehru margin 16–17% → ~20% ✅
Sangli plant June 2026 → August 2026 ❌
CFO/PAT → 0.43x ⚠

What to watch in FY27: Does Q4's margin recover above 22% in Q1 as hyperinflation accounting normalizes? Does Sangli August commissioning hold without further slippage? And do receivables finally convert or does the working capital drag persist?

Note: This is not investment advice.

Valuation Gravity: India’s Global Footprint Erodes

India’s share in global market capitalization has cratered to 2.9% following a brutal $180 billion rout over just two sessions. This retreat from a peak of 4.73% marks a four-year low and highlights a significant drawdown in investor confidence as the domestic mcap settles at $4.77 trillion. While the broader market remains the world's fifth-largest, the rapid deterioration suggests that the aggressive re-rating seen since 2020 has finally collided with fiscal reality.

The "friction point" is the aggressive cannibalization of market share by regional peers; Taiwan and South Korea are poised to overtake India following massive rallies of 45% and 75% respectively. This shift underscores a pivot away from India's expensive valuations and toward markets with heavy AI-linked exposure. The lack of large-scale AI plays and vulnerability to energy shocks have dulled the alpha, transforming a "clean" growth story into a liquidity trap for over-leveraged bulls.

BOTTOM LINE:
India’s mcap dominance has slipped into a dangerous trendline as regional competitors weaponize AI-linked growth. Failure to justify premium valuations against a -8.5% YTD performance risks a prolonged institutional exit toward more competitive tech-heavy markets.

The B2B Engine: India’s Silent Digital Arbitrage

Indian B2B e-commerce is scaling toward a USD 2.5 trillion market by FY30, yet digital penetration currently sits at a staggering sub-2%. This isn't just organic growth; it's a structural pivot where full-stack procurement platforms are capturing massive whitespaces. As institutional flows chase high-velocity QComm growth of 100% YoY, the real alpha is accumulating in these heavy-duty B2B channels that dwarf consumer-facing GMV.

The "friction point" lies in the disconnect between valuation hype and real-world adoption. While New-age logistics faces distress-driven consolidation, B2B e-commerce remains largely uncrowded despite its massive headroom. The common narrative focuses on the 270 million shoppers in consumer retail, but the industrial digital shift is where the unit economics are actually stabilizing, moving away from volume-at-all-costs toward disciplined pricing.

BOTTOM LINE:
The 2% digital penetration in a multi-trillion dollar B2B market represents the single largest structural trade in the region. Ignore the high-burn consumer noise; the institutional play is in the industrial digitisation and logistics consolidation.

🚨 **FROM DEFENCE APPROVALS TO BALANCE SHEET IMPROVEMENT — STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA LTD MAKING BIG MOVES**

**STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA LTD has secured a 5 Year Renewal Approval from Military Engineer Services (MES) under the Ministry of Defence**.

**Why is this important?** 👇

🔹 MES approvals are granted only to selected manufacturers meeting strict quality standards

🔹 Strengthens participation in Defence, Government & Infrastructure projects

🔹 SIMHADRI TMT approved for Fe 500D & Fe 500D HCRM/HSCRM TMT Bars

🔹 Advanced manufacturing using TEMPCORE technology

🔹 Fully integrated steel operations with in-house sponge iron, billet, rolling mill & power generation

📉 **Financial Strengthening**:
• Debt reduced by nearly ₹71.19 Cr
• ₹75 Cr capital infusion through convertible warrants

📊 **FY25 Snapshot:**
• Total Income: ₹1,163.37 Cr
• EBITDA: ₹143.60 Cr
• Net Profit: ₹25.93 Cr

🇮🇳 Also expanding into Specialty Steel under the PLI Scheme aligned with the Atmanirbhar Bharat vision.

🚨 **RURAL FINANCE PLAYER SHOWING STRONG MOMENTUM**

**AKME FINTRADE (INDIA) LTD. posted a solid FY26 performance with strong profitability growth, rising AUM and record disbursements.**

📊 **PERFORMANCE SNAPSHOT**

💰 PAT: ₹42.32 Cr
⬆️ Up 27.35% YoY

🔥 Q4 PAT: ₹12.27 Cr
⬆️ Up 62.67% YoY

🏦 AUM: ₹918.60 Cr
⬆️ Up 48.49% YoY

🚀 Disbursements: ₹503.91 Cr
🏆 Highest ever in company history

📈 NII: ₹86.19 Cr
⬆️ Up 37.14% YoY

⚡ **KEY GROWTH DRIVERS**
🚚 Vehicle Finance AUM surged 128% YoY
🌏 Strong focus on Tier II & Tier III markets
🏢 Presence across 5 states with 29 branches
👥 Customer base crossed 2,00,000+

🛡 FINANCIAL STRENGTH
✔️ CRAR at 46.23%
✔️ Gross NPA controlled at 2.93%
✔️ PCR above 52%
✔️ Credit Rating upgraded to A–

🔮 **FUTURE OUTLOOK**
⚙️ Focus on digital underwriting & collections
🚘 EV financing partnership with FINAYO
🪙 Expansion plans in insurance & gold loans

📌 **Strong rural penetration + diversified growth strategy continue to support long term expansion story.**

Clean Max Enviro Energy Solutions guides for strong growth ahead:

To add 1.5 GW new capacity in FY27

Net debt at ₹10,000 Cr, cost of debt reduced to 8.5%

EBITDA expected to rise to ₹2200–2500 Cr by FY28 vs ₹1295 Cr currently

Forex Shields Under Fire

India’s forex reserves have shrunk by 5.2% since March 2026, dropping from $728.5 billion to $690.7 billion by May 1. As crude prices surge, the RBI has burned through billions to defend a rupee that recently touched a life-time low of 95.62 against the dollar. This depletion is among the highest for oil-importing emerging markets, leaving the currency increasingly vulnerable.

While other EMs like Hungary and Chile have managed to conserve or even grow their resources, India faces a double whammy of capital outflows and high import costs. Even with a brief recovery in April, sustained pressure in the final weeks has forced the central bank to tighten curbs on banks' dollar exposure. The lesson? A massive war chest provides protection, but even the deepest pockets have limits when geopolitical shocks hit the energy basket this hard.

NUVAMA ON TATA POWER

Downgrade to Reduce from Hold with a target price of ₹390 (earlier 388)

Module strength cushions Genco drag 

Discoms/TP solar growth offset by RE Genco/Solar EPC drag

RE capacity addition and new initiatives to drive PAT by FY28E

At CMP, find most positives largely  priced in and foresee growth to be back ended

Remain constructive on company's long-term story, baking in CGPL resolution

INOX INDIA

Niche Business getting into another niche segment

Building solutions for ISRO's Space Mission

When returns are good, almost anyone can invest!

Just look at the SIP ageing data across different periods.

Between March 2022 and March 2023, markets were rallying and Direct Plan SIPs (137% growth) were growing faster than Regular Plan SIPs (63% growth).

Leverage is pro-cyclical i.e., increase as the markets go up, which has been the case in the post-pandemic period. While leverage in F&O has steadily reduced because of regulatory tightening, some of it has migrated to margin trading facility (MTF).

MTF's popularity is very recent and most retail customers don't really understand the risks. While we offer MTF, we do it without being loud about it or constantly pushing it. We are probably the only platform to warn users before they enable MTF.

**INDIA UREA – IMPORT DEPENDENCY SHIFT**
• Urea import dependency fell from 86% in FY22 to 28% in FY25
• FY22 spike driven by global fertiliser supply disruptions
• Domestic players doubled market share from 11% to 22%
• Higher local production sharply reduced imports
• India moving towards urea self-sufficiency
**Impact:** Positive for domestic fertiliser manufacturers & import substitution theme

reports.text:
**JPM INDIA EQUITY STRATEGY — FACTOR INVESTING UPDATE (MAY 2026) 📊🇮🇳**

**Market View**
- JPM downgraded Indian equities to Neutral within Asia  
- Cited:
  - Slowing cyclical recovery  
  - Macro headwinds  
  - Middle East geopolitical risks  
  - Oil and trade disruption concerns  

**Earnings Outlook**
- CY26 MSCI India EPS growth estimate cut to 11%  
- CY27 MSCI India EPS growth estimate cut to 13%  
- FY27 sector earnings estimates reduced by 2–10% across key sectors  

**Factor Strategy**
- QMI positioned in “Mid” cycle phase  
- Preferred factors:
  - Momentum (earnings-led)  
  - Quality  

- Avoid:
  - Value factor  

**Market Cap Preference**
- Smallcaps and midcaps preferred during mid-cycle phase  
- YTD Performance:
  - Smallcaps: +2.0%  
  - Midcaps: -1.3%  
  - Nifty 50: -8.0%  

**Sector Preferences**
- Top overweight sectors:
  - Financials  
  - Materials  

**Macro Commentary**
- Cyclical recovery losing momentum even before oil shock  
- Middle East tensions continue impacting:
  - Oil prices  
  - Trade routes  
  - Global growth sentiment  
  - Earnings visibility  

**Final Takeaway**
- JPM’s strategy note signals a more cautious stance on Indian equities amid slowing earnings momentum and rising macro uncertainty. Preference is shifting toward quality and earnings momentum plays rather than deep value opportunities, while financials and materials remain preferred sectors in the current market cycle  

**Impact**
- Neutral to Cautious Near-Term  

@Stockinfo333

Morgan Stanley On Dr Reddy

Target Price ₹1215

Earlier Target ₹1259

Recommendation Equal-weight

Weak Q4; Semaglutide ramp-up key to watch

Adjusted revenues and EBITDA were 2% and 13% below estimates

Semaglutide ramp-up is slighlty delayed, awaiting Brazil approval

Expects 6/7million pen sales in CY26

In FY28, to see 40 million+ capacity

Risk of high competition in semaglutide and in-line valuations keep us Equal-weight

Citi On Dr Reddy

Target Price ₹1070

Recommendation Sell

Reality check on numbers as gRevlimid tailwinds fade

US sales have fallen at pre-gRevlimid levels, indicating erosion in the base-line products

Core profitability and US revenues remain under pressure

Strong growth in non-US markets appears increasingly cost-funded and dilutive to margins

Jefferies On DR Reddy

Target Price ₹1040

Recommendation Underperform

Q4 miss even after adjusting for one-offs

Margins declined on weak US performance and high SG&A even though India, Russia and Europe delivered growth

Management guided for double-digit year-on-year revenue growth in FY27 ex gRevlimid

Slower uptake of semaglutide in Canada and delay in launch of Abatacept pose downside risks to our estimate.

Citi On Max Financial

Target Price ₹2725

Earlier Target ₹2675

Recommendation Buy

Another strong quarter

Quarter-on-quarter margin improvement was led by higher operating leverage benefits and likely narrowing of ITC-led drag

Positive operating variance provides comfort on back book

Persistency trends held up well across buckets

Expect growth trajectory to sustain at higher than industry level for private players

Investor interest in proposed QIP remains monitorable

Valuation remains benign

Jefferies on Grasim

Target Price ₹3440

Recommendation Buy

Grasim is set to receive Rs 3970 crore from UltraTech - a sharp step-up versus prior years

Past 2 years suggest Grasim has retained half of the dividend to fund new businesses

With dividend pool structurally looking higher, Grasim now has greater capital availability

See optionality across growth funding, deleveraging, & shareholder returns

If executed prudently, scope to narrow the HoldCo discount

BofA On CPI

April CPI review: No pressures visible yet

CPI Details: Food prices higher, services prices contained

Pass-through of elevated prices was visible in March WPI

RBI April MPM Minutes echoed the cautious tone

Continue to expect a cumulative 50 bps hike starting December 2026

Strong business momentum backed by healthy execution, expansion plans and rising demand for industrial automation solutions across key sectors.

Park Medi World Limited delivered a strong performance in FY26, marked by consistent growth and improved financial strength. The company reported a 20.5% year-on-year increase in consolidated revenue to ₹16,793.56 Mn, while PAT grew by 27% to ₹2,735.57 Mn, reflecting enhanced profitability and operating efficiency.

In Q4 FY26, momentum further accelerated, with revenue rising 30.1% YoY and EBITDA and PAT growing by 44% and 46.6% respectively, supported by margin expansion.

The year was also defined by strategic milestones, including a successful IPO, aggressive acquisitions, and significant deleveraging, reducing debt by 95%. Strong operating cash flows and capacity expansion initiatives position the company well for sustained growth, reinforcing its trajectory as a scalable healthcare platform.

Global Copper Supply-Demand Model

Copper Supply and Demand Outlook

The copper market is expected to enter a period of growing deficits due to significant supply constraints and increasing global demand. 

Jefferies' supply and demand model forecasts that total global copper demand will grow at a CAGR of 2.1% from 2025 to 2030E, reaching 30,931 kt by 2030, while total supply is projected to grow at a slower CAGR of 1.7%, reaching 30,136 kt in the same period. 

This imbalance is expected to result in a widening deficit, with a notional deficit of (795 kt) projected by 2030E.

Deep dive on the fiber opportunity

Global fiber production is around 570 million fiber-kilometres annually, with APAC dominating output, but US hyperscalers increasingly require domestically produced fiber due to CHIPS Act incentives, geopolitical risks, and supply chain audits. 

Projects like Meta’s Hyperion data center in Louisiana require 12.9 million fkm of fiber, highlighting the scale of demand, with estimates suggesting half of US fiber capacity may be needed for AI infrastructure by 2027.

**THE HIDDEN INFRASTRUCTURE LAYER BEHIND AI, 5G, EVs & INDUSTRIAL AUTOMATION ⚡️📡🤖**

**The Under-Tracked Opportunity**
- One of the most overlooked infrastructure layers behind:
  - AI data centres  
  - 5G telecom rollout  
  - EV ecosystem  
  - Industrial automation  
  - Defence electronics  
  - Semiconductor manufacturing  

- Core enabler is:
  - Programmable AC/DC power systems  
  - Industrial testing infrastructure  
  - Precision validation equipment  

- Global rack-mount power supply market estimated at ~$8.6Bn in 2025 and projected to exceed ~$12Bn by 2030  

---

**1. AI Data Centres & Hyperscale Compute – Largest Demand Driver**
- AI hyperscale deployments reportedly contribute nearly 55% of industry demand  
- Every AI server rack and GPU cluster requires:
  - PSU validation  
  - Harmonic analysis  
  - Surge simulation  
  - Frequency testing  
  - Battery backup testing  
  - Rack power reliability checks  

- Modern AI infrastructure depends on:
  - N+1 redundancy  
  - Hot-swappable power modules  
  - Precision voltage regulation  
  - Remote monitoring systems  

- AI-related programmable power demand reportedly growing at ~11%+ CAGR globally  
- Hidden opportunity lies not only in GPUs and servers, but in the testing and power ecosystem enabling stable deployments  

---

**2. Telecom & 5G Infrastructure**
- Telecom and 5G contribute nearly 18% industry share  
- Massive testing opportunity emerging across:
  - BTS racks  
  - Base stations  
  - mmWave systems  
  - Satellite communication infrastructure  
  - Telecom export equipment  

- Programmable AC sources and frequency converters critical because telecom equipment must comply with:
  - US 60Hz standards  
  - European 50Hz systems  
  - Aircraft-grade 400Hz environments  

- India’s accelerating 5G rollout could structurally increase telecom-grade testing demand over the next several years  

---

**3. Industrial Automation & EV Ecosystem**
- Industrial automation and EV-related demand contribute around 12% share globally  
- Key growth areas include:
  - Robotics  
  - Servo drives  
  - Solar inverters  
  - EV battery systems  
  - Factory automation  

- EV battery testing becoming a major long-term demand driver for programmable DC systems used in:
  - Charge-discharge simulation  
  - Thermal stress testing  
  - Fast charging validation  
  - Motor controller testing  
  - BMS reliability testing  

- Industry 4.0 transition is steadily increasing requirement for precision testing infrastructure  

---

**4. Defence & Aerospace – Smallest Segment, Highest Moat**
- Defence and aerospace contribute only ~5% market share but often generate highest margins  
- Ruggedised programmable power systems used in:
  - Radar electronics  
  - Missile systems  
  - Avionics  
  - Military-grade testing  
  - Harsh industrial environments  

- Industry characteristics include:
  - Long approval cycles  
  - High qualification barriers  
  - Extremely low vendor substitution risk  
  - Sticky customer relationships  

- Specialized ruggedised systems reportedly among the fastest-growing high-value categories globally  

---

**5. Healthcare, Research Labs & Semiconductor Ecosystem**
- Another underappreciated demand layer includes:
  - MRI & CT testing systems  
  - Semiconductor characterization  
  - IIT/ISRO/CSIR lab infrastructure  
  - Medical device compliance systems  
  - Energy storage research  

- As India deepens focus on:
  - Semiconductor manufacturing  
  - Aerospace  
  - Electronics manufacturing  
  - Strategic R&D  

- Precision programmable power systems could become increasingly critical infrastructure  

---

**Industry Shift – Hardware to Intelligent Engineering Platforms**
- Industry rapidly evolving from conventional standalone power supplies toward:
  - Smart programmable systems  
  - IoT-connected architectures  
  - Remote-controlled infrastructure  
  - AI-monitored testing ecosystems  
  - Real-time telemetry & diagnostics  

- Programmable DC systems with remote monitoring already represent significant portion of

(1) sustained market share accretion across most insurance segments backed by product innovation, vertical integration (ex. claims management, improving the underwriting journey of manufacturers, garage network, etc.) and ramp up of tech capabilities,

Arfin India Ltd | Q4 FY26 & FY26 Result Highlights

Arfin India reported a strong Q4 FY26 performance, led by better profitability and sharp margin expansion.

Q4 FY26 Performance
• Revenue from Operations: ₹19,323.76 lakhs
↳ Up 2.81% QoQ and 26.00% YoY

• EBITDA: ₹1,632.90 lakhs
↳ Up 17.58% QoQ and 147.98% YoY

↳ Up 34.04% QoQ and 1,180.60% YoY

FY26 vs FY25

• EBITDA: ₹4,616.13 lakhs vs ₹3,630.01 lakhs — 27.17% growth
• EBITDA Margin: 7.47% vs 5.90% — up 157 bps
• PAT: ₹1,545.13 lakhs vs ₹914.63 lakhs — 68.93% growth

✅ Key takeaway:
Revenue remained largely stable on a full-year basis, but EBITDA and PAT grew strongly, showing better operating efficiency, improved margins and stronger earnings quality.

**COPPER – THE METAL AT THE CENTER OF THE NEXT INDUSTRIAL SUPER CYCLE**

**Why Copper Matters**
- Multiple global megatrends are converging on one critical metal:
  - AI infrastructure  
  - Electric vehicles  
  - Renewable energy  
  - Grid modernization  
  - Defence infrastructure  

- Copper remains extremely difficult to substitute due to:
  - High electrical conductivity  
  - Thermal efficiency  
  - Durability  
  - Reliability across industrial applications  

**1. AI Data Center Demand**
- Every 1 MW of AI data center capacity requires ~27 tonnes of copper  

- Copper heavily used across:
  - Power distribution systems  
  - Transformers  
  - Cooling systems  
  - Networking infrastructure  
  - High-density cabling  
  - Grid connectivity  

- Global data center copper demand projected to rise:
  - ~1.1 million MT (2025)  
  - ~2.5 million MT by 2040  

- AI training infrastructure could contribute ~58% of total data center copper demand by 2030  

- AI is increasingly becoming:
  - A power infrastructure story  
  - A grid expansion story  
  - A metals demand story  

**2. Electric Vehicles (EVs)**
- EVs use ~2.9x more copper than ICE vehicles  

- Copper required in:
  - Electric motors  
  - Battery systems  
  - Wiring harnesses  
  - Inverters  
  - Charging systems  
  - Power electronics  

- EV-related copper demand projected to increase:
  - ~1.7 million MT (2025)  
  - ~4.3 million MT by 2035  

- Additional copper demand also coming from:
  - EV charging networks  
  - Battery gigafactories  
  - Transmission infrastructure  

**3. Renewable Energy Transition**
- Renewable systems require ~2.5x–7x more copper versus fossil fuel systems  

- Copper is critical for:
  - Solar farms  
  - Wind turbines  
  - Inverters  
  - Transformers  
  - Battery energy storage  
  - Grid integration  

- AI-led power demand is also indirectly accelerating renewable infrastructure investments globally  

**4. Global Grid Modernization**
- IEA expects global electricity demand to rise ~50% by 2040  

- Massive investments expected in:
  - Transmission lines  
  - HVDC infrastructure  
  - Smart grids  
  - Substations  
  - Distribution modernization  
  - Electrification systems  

- Copper remains foundational across nearly every layer of modern power infrastructure  

**5. Defence & Strategic Infrastructure**
- Rising geopolitical tensions increasing defence spending globally  

- Copper demand rising across:
  - Advanced electronics  
  - Naval electrification  
  - Radar systems  
  - Aerospace systems  
  - Secure communications  
  - Military-grade power systems  

- Copper increasingly becoming a strategic sovereignty metal  

**The Real Challenge: Supply Constraints**
- New copper mines typically require 10–15+ years to develop  

- Structural supply risks include:
  - Declining ore grades  
  - Tougher permitting norms  
  - ESG constraints  
  - Geopolitical concentration risks  

- This creates possibility of long-duration structural supply tightness  

**India’s Copper Ecosystem**

- Precision Wires India Limited  
  - Leader in magnet wires  
  - Beneficiary of EV, transformer and renewable demand growth  

- Ram Ratna Wires Limited  
  - Exposure to wires, HVAC and EV ecosystem growth  

- Hindustan Copper Limited  
  - India’s only integrated copper miner  
  - Targeting ~3x capacity expansion by FY31  

- Hindalco Industries Limited  
  - Operates Birla Copper smelting business at Dahej  

- Adani Enterprises Limited  
  - Developing Kutch Copper project with large-scale smelting ambitions  

- Cubex Tubings Limited  
  - Niche copper tubes and rods player  

**Core Structural Theme**
- Copper is increasingly evolving from a cyclical industrial commodity into:
  - An AI infrastructure metal  
  - An electrification metal  
  - An energy transition metal  
  - A defence infrastructure metal  
  - A strategic resource for nations  

**Final Takeaway**
- The next decade’s infrastructure buildout across AI, EVs, renewables, grids and defence could create one of the strongest long-duration structural

reports.text:
Jefferies on Titan

Buy, TP Rs 4800

Jewellery business reported strong revenue growth - while higher gold prices helped, buyer growth rebound was a key positive, along with smart growth in studded.

Jewellery margin continued to slide, but we think is less relevant now.

1HFY27 should continue to benefit from gold price inflation, with a medium-term target of 15-20% CAGR.

Watches also had a strong qtr.

Overall, EBITDA was slightly below due to staff cost provision.

Valuation Snapshot of SaaS Companies

src : JM Financials

CITI on Titan

Neutral, TP Rs 5075

Standalone jewellery revenue growth (ex-bullion) was strong at 45% YoY (2% above Citi est).

However, jewellery margin contracted by 135bps YoY to 10.5% (vs 11.5% Citi est); Domestic TMZ EBIT margin declined 60bps YoY to 11.3%.

Management highlighted:

(a) standalone margin had impact of Rs800mn for transfer pricing pertaining to sales to international subsidiaries;

(b) likely gained 50-60bps market share in FY26;

(c) led by gold price increase, there was some advancement of wedding purchases.

While near-term growth outlook remains strong, sustained increase in gold prices might have preponed consumer demand and to that extent growth in FY27E will be key to sustaining current valuations

JPM on BoB

OW TP Rs 335

4QFY26 core operating performance came better vs JPMe with broad-based beats across NIM, recoveries and opex.

NII/PPOP/PAT came 2%/11%/6% above JPMe respectively.

RAM (retail, agri and MSME) segments were the key growth drivers with 5.9%/6.3%/4.2% q/q growth respectively with share rising to 61% of domestic advances (vs 59.8% y/y) with growth focus likely to remain high in this segment for granular as well as NIM accretive growth.

Deposit growth (+12% y/y) lagged loan growth (+16.5% y/y).

However, BOB does not see this as a growth constraint and expects loan growth of 12-14% in FY27 (in line, JPMe 13.5%).

Jefferies on BoB

Hold, TP Rs 295

Q4 profit of Rs56bn, up 11% YoY, was ahead of est and included many non-core gains that were partly used to make floating provisions.

Loans grew by 16% and NII growth of 9% included some interest on tax refund.

Asset quality and core credit costs have been stable.

LDR of 83% and bulk deposit share at 23% of domestic deposit is higher than PSU peers.

Kotak Inst Eqt on BOB

ADD, TP Rs 300

BoB reported 10% yoy earnings growth, although several line items that are usually hard to forecast drove the better-than-expected performance.

NIM increased 10 bps qoq.

Slippages stood at 1%, with management comfortable that the Middle East exposure remains manageable at this stage.

Barring adverse macro-outcomes, management expressed confidence in raising FY2027 growth guidance while maintaining operating ratios unchanged.

CITI on MGL

Buy, TP Rs 1400

MGL reported 4Q EBITDA at Rs2.6bn (-26% qoq), in line with estimates.

While volumes were slightly ahead (+6% yoy), this was offset by slightly weaker-than-expected margins.

Reported net income at Rs1.3bn (-35% qoq) was also largely in line.

FY26 EPS was Rs86/sh (FY25: Rs105/sh).

MGL declared a final divi. of Rs18/sh (full year divi. of Rs30/sh).

While LNG supply disruptions linked to Middle East tensions continue to pose risks to near-term volumes & margins, recent gov’t policy initiatives remain supportive of longer-term CGD sector growth, keeping us positively inclined on stock

MS on Swiggy

EW,  TP Rs 322

Key highlights,

1) Slight relative market share gain based on GOV metrics of FD in 4Q,

2) QC growth in F27 likely to see a sharp deceleration,

3) Management maintains break even at contribution margin (CM) level in QC business in 1Q (MSe assumes 2QF27).

Kotak Inst Eqt on Swiggy

Buy, TP Rs 370

Swiggy’s food delivery GOV growth in 4Q came in at 23% yoy & ahead of estimates.

Instamart’s GOV grew 67% yoy but decelerated to flat growth qoq; NOV growth was a tad better at 3.6% qoq.

Instamart’s contribution margin improved from (-)2.5% in 3Q to (-)1.8% in 4Q, though absolute EBITDA loss remained high at Rs8.6 bn

Believe that Instamart may see further growth pressures & a scenario of weak GOV growth, and high absolute losses (CM break-even notwithstanding) may persist

Trim Instamart revenue and EBITDA estimates & ascribe a valuation of Rs69 bn (Rs111 bn earlier) to this business.

CLSA on BOB

O-P, TP Rs 335

4Q PBT in line but had several one-offs including interest on IT refund, employee provision reversals and floating provisions.

Core performance was mixed.

Loan growth picked up to 16.5% YoY & better than that of many banks.

Deposit growth marginally picked up to 12% YoY, while CASA ratio improved slightly QoQ.

While reported NIM improved 10bp QoQ, believe it was driven by interest on IT refund.
Core NIM, would have declined.

Loan yields declined 12bp QoQ due to the Dec 2025 repo rate cut, while cost of deposits hardened by 3bp.

Fee income declined YoY despite healthy loan growth performance.

Management has guided to 2.75-2.95% NIMs in FY27 and expects similar interest on IT refunds through FY27 as well.

CITI on BoB

Buy, TP Rs 340

4Q PAT of Rs56.2bn (RoA: 1.15%) was materially aided by one-offs —IT refund of Rs12.9bn, recoveries from written-off accounts of Rs14.85bn, dividend income of Rs2.9bn, and a Rs2.2bn reversal of retirement benefit provisions.

Reported NIMs expanded 10bps QoQ to 2.89%, though was largely IT-refund-driven; stripping this out, YoAs contracted ~12bps while CoDs crept up 3bps — underscoring persistent core NIM pressure

Bank proactively created Rs15bn of floating provisions as a buffer against global macro headwinds.

Management revised FY27 loan growth guidance upward to 12–14% (vs. 11–13% prior), with NIMs guided at 2.75–2.95% (incl benefit of int on IT refund), credit cost below 60bps, and RoA above 1%.

Bernstein on SBI

Market Perform, TP Rs 1300

SBI delivered a mixed 4Q26

Loan growth accelerated to 17% YoY but NIM saw a sharp 18 bps QoQ decline to 2.93%, steepest compression amongst large banks.

Slippages rose sequentially, though largely seasonal, while headline GNPA improved to 1.49%.

RoA moderated 12 bps QoQ to 1.07%, weighed down by NIM compression & a Treasury MTM loss.

Management retained FY27 guidance of 13–15% credit growth, >3% domestic NIM & 50 bps credit costs

Management reaffirmed its through-cycle commitment to 1%+ RoA & 15%+ RoE

UBS on SBI 

Neutral, TP Rs 1080

NII declined QoQ as margins dip 18bp QoQ due to loan pricing cut

Loan growth at c5% QoQ led by Corporates; asset quality stable

Management guides for loan growth of 13-15% for FY27; credit cost of c50bp

Expect bank's ROA to see downward pressure as treasury gains and credit costs normalize while margin trends remain subdued

Thus estimate ROA of 0.95% over FY27-28E (vs c1.12% in FY26) & believe risk reward at current valuations (1.2x FY27E P/BV) seems fair

Kotak Inst Eqt on SBI

Buy, TP Rs 1250

SBI reported about 5% yoy earnings growth, led by a 55% yoy decline in provisions, while operating profits declined about 10% yoy

Bank reported solid loan growth (~17% yoy), low credit costs (~25 bps) and a NIM decline of 20 bps qoq

Miss against expectations is likely to prompt a near-term pause in its re-rating, with investors awaiting evidence of recovery before rerating bank.

CLSA on Lupin

O-P TP Rs 2690

An all-round beat in 4QFY26, driven by strong US business, higher India profitability and better operating efficiency

Co guided for high-single-digit to low-double-digit revenue growth in FY27 due to expected competition in Mirabegron and Tolvaptan.

Ebitda margin is expected to be 25% due to product mix, increased R&D spend and cost pressures on account of the Iran war.

India business is expected to outperform IPM by 1.2-1.3x, driven by a strong sales force and 80 upcoming product launches

CLSA on Hyundai

O-P, TP cut to Rs 2290

4Q FY26 Ebitda margin of 10.4%, missing estimate by 70bps & down 84bps QoQ.

Margins were dented by a one-off 50-60bps vendor compensation hit, a 40bps impact from labour codes, commodity inflation &  an adverse mix (SUV mix -600bps, export mix -500bps).

This was partly offset by lower discounts (1.9% of ASP vs 2.6% in 3Q), operating leverage, a 0.6% price hike and state incentives (~50bps in our view).

HMIL guides 8-10% volume growth in FY27, supported by a capacity ramp-up and two new nameplate launches (a compact e-SUV and a mid-size SUV).

While commodity headwinds persist, management remains confident of sustaining 11-14% Ebitda margin in FY27.

BoFA Sec on Pidilite

With 40-50% input cost inflation, margin could head to the low-end of guided range, despite two price hikes already taken.

Price elasticity and competition remain other watchouts

Moreover, stock valuation leaves no upside

OW, TP 1225

SBI reported a weaker than expected 4Q with NII (+3.8% y/y, -2% q/q) at Rs 443.8bn moderating vs +9% y/y in 3Q26 and coming 6% below JPM.

SBI recorded a 17bps q/q decline in NIM (whole bank) to 2.81% and an 18bps q/q decline in NIM (domestic) to 2.93%, vs. Street expectations of flattish to marginally lower margins q/q. However, SBI remains confident of NIM recovery in FY27 vs 4Q backed by multiple steps highlighted below.

Asset quality was benign with credit costs (on avg loans) at 24bps (-16bps q/q), although slippages (+3bps q/q) edged up slightly on seasonality.

O-P TP Rs 1275

However, on top line, it was a weak quarter, with NII missing estimates by 5%.

This was driven by a 17bp sequential compression in NIM, sharper than expected

Apart from NIM, it was a good quarter.

Loan growth of 17% YoY was best in class, while deposit growth picked up from 9% to 11% YoY

Goldman Sachs India Strategy

Outflows Fade, But Re-entry Waits

Foreign ownership has retreated to historical lows, below DIIs

Petroleum coke - could a little-known raw material be another big supply risk for aluminium? Good Read From JPM

The global aluminium market faces heightened supply risks due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting both alumina and petroleum coke (pet coke) supplies. 

Pet coke, a critical raw material for carbon anodes in aluminium smelting, has seen only a +21% price increase despite ~20% of its supply being impacted by the conflict. 

While no major shortages have been reported yet, producers like Alcoa and Norsk Hydro note rising carbon-related costs and supply uncertainty. 

The aluminium industry remains dependent on pet coke in the near term, as inert anode technologies (e.g., ELYSIS, HalZero) are still in development.

Food delivery business expected to generate Rs 1,400-2,200 crore FCF in FY27-28

Medium-term target of Rs 1 trillion GOV with 3-4% EBITDA margin

Stock seen undervaluing QC business but execution on profitability remains key

Nomura On Hyundai Motor India

Target Price ₹2,407

Recommendation Buy

Strong growth guidance despite tough environment

Revenue and Ebitda miss estimates

Miss led by labour code and higher material costs

Domestic growth guided at 8-10% for FY27; Exports at 8-10% growth

argin guidance FY27 margin guidance at 11-14%, indicating margins likely bottomed out

Nomura On Lupin

Target Price ₹2580

Recommendation Buy

Sales/EBITDA/PAT were above estimates

India formulations aspires to grow 1.2-1.3x the broader market

US revenues can decline in high-single-digit to low-double-digits in FY27E

Capital allocation and acquisition: Specialty and India are key focus areas

@Stocksip

Macquarie On Bank of Baroda

Target Price ₹280

Recommendation Neutral

4Q26 PAT beat driven by lower operating costs

NIM at 2.89% above estimates, supported by tax refund benefit

Non-interest income 11% below estimates due to MTM losses

FY27 NIM guided at 2.75-2.95% with near-term pressure from deposit costs

Macquarie On Escorts Kubota

Target Price ₹3777

Recommendation Outperform

Q1FY27 demand expected to be strong on low base

Q4FY26 EBITDA 3% below estimates

Flattish year-on-year farm segment margin a disappointment

Margins remain at risk due to input cost pressures

Expansion plans on track with land acquisition likely in FY27

Macquarie On Hyundai Motor India

Target Price ₹2235

Recommendation Outperform

Muted quarter; new launches to drive growth

EBITDA margin at 10.4%, with FY27 guidance at 11-14%

each for domestic Management guides 8-10% growth

Growth to be driven by new launches, pricing, mix and operating leverage

Must visit 
@Stockupdate9

Macquarie On Lupin

Target Price ₹2,450

Recommendation Outperform

Q4FY26 results beat estimates

Pipeline strong with 52 FTF opportunities including 22 exclusives

Targeting 5+ biosimilar launches by FY31

10+ ANDA filings done in FY26 and 15+ planned for FY27

Macquarie On SBI

Target Price ₹1150

Recommendation Outperform

PAT missed estimates

Weak margins spooks confidence

Earnings miss driven by weak margins and trading losses

NIM impacted by higher proportion of floating book

FY27 NIM guidance ~3% with expectation of yield stabilisation and better CASA mix

FY27 loan growth guidance maintained at 13-15%

Asset quality remains a bright spot

Morgan Stanley On Bank of Baroda

Target Price ₹225

Recommendation Underweight

Q4: Good Quarter

Core revenues were 2% above estimates

Core PPOP was 23% above estimates due to reversal of retirement provisions

This and lower tax provisions and bad loan recoveries drove a PAT beat of 27%

Stock could do well in the near term

But stay Underweight given low core RoA and macro risks

Citi On Balkrishna

Target Price ₹2100

Earlier Target ₹2050

Recommendation Sell

QrFY26 EBITDA slightly below estimate

Healthy volumes offset by escalated costs

PAT miss amplified by negative other income

Outlook is positive over the medium to longer term

HBSC On Titan

Target Price ₹4,930

Earlier Target ₹4,510

Recommendation Buy

Strong Q4 with underlying strength in its jewellery business despite rising gold prices

Headline revenue beat was supported by high bullion sales

Raises FY27-28 EPS estimates by 3-5% on strong jewellery performance

Company expects strong H1FY27 growth of over 30%

H2FY27 growth likely to moderate due to high base uncertain gold price

JPMorgan On Dabur

Target Price ₹525

Earlier Target ₹470

Recommendation Neutral

Q4 in-line with revenue 1% above estimates; EBITDA on target

Management guides low double-digit revenue growth for FY27

Margins expected flat-to-improving

EPS estimates for FY27-28 raised on higher revenue growth and better margin

valuations now supportive

Macquarie On Avlon Tehnologies

Target Price ₹1,350

Earlier Target ₹1,130

Recommendation Outperform

Q4FY26 strong; Revenue and PAT Beat estimates

EBITDA margin at 11.8%, below estimates and down 21 bps year-on-year

Growth led by clean energy, industrials and mobility segments

FY27 revenue growth guidance conservative at 24-27% year-on-year

Company had guided 20% earlier for FY26 but delivered 46%, indicating upside risk
